evals.prompt: I have a Next.js SaaS project and want to set up QA project context from scratch
expected_patterns: .agents/qa-project-context.md created
Product section with name and URLs
Tech Stack section with Next.js details
Test Stack section
CI/CD section
Quality Goals section
Risk Areas table with at least 3 entries
Team section
anti_patterns: Risk Areas section empty or missing

evals.prompt: Set up QA context for my project. I have Playwright and Jest already, running on GitHub Actions
expected_patterns: playwright.config.ts detected OR ask about Playwright version
jest.config.js detected OR ask about Jest setup
.github/workflows/ detected as CI/CD platform
Test Stack section populated with Playwright and Jest
CI/CD section with GitHub Actions config location
anti_patterns: asking about Playwright when playwright.config.ts exists
generic CI/CD section without GitHub Actions specifics

evals.prompt: My .agents/qa-project-context.md already exists but has some [bracketed placeholders]. Update only the empty sections
expected_patterns: read existing file first
identify sections with placeholders
ask only about unfilled sections
preserve completed sections
remove all [brackets] from final file

evals.prompt: I need to identify critical risk areas for my e-commerce platform. What questions should you ask to fill the Risk Areas section?
expected_patterns: Which parts cause production incidents
Flakiest integrations (payment, email, third-party APIs)
High churn + low coverage areas
Impact x Likelihood framework mentioned
Risk table with Area, Risk Level, Business Impact, Notes columns
anti_patterns: Risk table with fewer than 3 entries

evals.prompt: I'm a solo developer with no QA team. How should the Team section be filled for my context?
expected_patterns: acknowledgement of high dev/QA ratio (solo = infinity)
recommendation that developers must write tests
QA focuses on strategy not manual execution
Test Stack reflects low-barrier setup (Playwright, Vitest, etc)
Team section explicitly states actual headcount

evals.prompt: My project is a monorepo with 3 separate frontend apps and a shared API. How should I structure the Tech Stack and Test Stack sections?
expected_patterns: multiple frontend entries in Tech Stack section
shared backend/API entry
test directories per app OR shared test infrastructure
turbo.json OR pnpm-workspace.yaml mentioned as detection hint
separate test sharding strategy for each app if applicable
anti_patterns: flat Tech Stack without distinguishing components
unclear which tests run for which app

evals.prompt: My team has Playwright E2E tests but no unit tests yet. What should the Test Stack section say, and which skill should I use next?
expected_patterns: Test Stack: E2E Framework Playwright (with version, config, directory)
Test Stack: Unit Framework 'None selected yet' OR 'Not yet implemented'
no [brackets] or placeholder text
suggest unit-testing skill as next step
note Vitest as modern default for unit testing
anti_patterns: leaving 'TBD' or [unit framework] in Test Stack
no guidance on what to do next

evals.prompt: Set up quality goals section for my early-stage startup. What realistic coverage and speed targets should I include?
expected_patterns: 60% unit coverage target OR similar early-stage suggestion
E2E for top 5 critical flows
<3 minute unit test duration target
<15 minute E2E suite duration target
flakiness threshold <2%
concrete measurable goals (numbers, percentages, durations)
anti_patterns: aspirational goals like 'we want great quality'

evals.prompt: How do I fill the Conventions section for selector strategy if my team uses semantic selectors (ARIA roles)?
expected_patterns: Selector Strategy: ARIA roles and semantic HTML mentioned
example selectors like role='button' or role='heading'
recommendation for accessibility-aware testing
note on stability vs. data-testid tradeoff
naming convention or pattern documented

evals.prompt: When I say my context file is done, what validations should confirm it's complete and ready for other skills to use?
expected_patterns: no [bracketed placeholders] remain
all 9 sections present (Product, Tech Stack, Test Stack, CI/CD, Environments, Quality Goals, Risk Areas, Team, Conventions)
Risk Areas table has at least 3 entries
Product section includes 5+ key user flows
Team section shows actual headcount or 'solo'
Quality Goals are concrete with numbers/percentages
anti_patterns: vague user flows like 'user uses app'
